title: Veggie Lo Mein
description: This 20-Minute Veggie Lo Mein Bowl is perfect if you need a quick and healthy vegetarian dinner idea!
ingredients: 3.5 ounces shiitake mushrooms, stemmed and thinly sliced
1 red bell pepper, seeded and thinly sliced
1/4 teaspoon fine sea salt, plus more to taste
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rapid boil. Cook the 8 ounces whole-wheat spaghetti until just shy of al dente, 8 to 12 minutes, then drain. You want the noodles slightly undercooked since they will finish cooking in the skillet.

While the spaghetti cooks, whisk together the sauce in a small bowl: 1/3 cup reduced-sodium tamari, 1 tablespoon grated fresh ginger, 1 tablespoon honey, 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil, and 2 teaspoons sriracha. Set aside.

Heat 1 tablespoon olive or avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the 3.5 ounces sliced shiitake mushrooms, 1 sliced red bell pepper, 1 cup grated carrots, and 3 minced garlic cloves. Cook, stirring frequently, until tender, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add the 3 cups broccoli florets and 1 cup snow peas to the skillet and toss to combine. Season with 1/4 teaspoon fine sea salt and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes.

Add the drained noodles and prepared sauce to the skillet. Toss everything together until noodles and vegetables are evenly coated. Taste and add more salt if desired. Serve garnished with sliced green onions.
